name: Pears with Spicy Lime, Chile and Peanut Chutney
recipeIngredient: 5 teaspoons Lime juice (from one lime), plus zest
1/2 cup Peanuts, dry roasted and finely ground
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut each pear lengthwise into quarters.
2. Using a melon baller or grapefruit spoon, create a well in the center of each quarter. If the pears are very small, use halves instead of quarters.
3. In a small bowl, prepare the chutney by mixing together lime juice and zest, red pepper flakes, garlic, soy sauce, brown sugar, and peanuts until combined.
4. Spoon the prepared chutney into the hollowed-out section of each pear quarter.
5. Add a sprig of mint for garnish.
6. Place on a serving tray.
Helpful hint: The chutney can be prepared up to one week in advance. To avoid browning, cut the pears no earlier than a few hours before serving; then sprinkle with lime juice.
